summary: Chainlink (LINK) conducted a public token sale in September 2017 and distributed tokens to the team and node operator reserve at launch with NO on-chain time-locks or smart-contract-enforced vesting schedules. The entire 1,000,000,000 LINK supply was minted at genesis and allocated across three buckets (35% public sale, 35% node operator incentives held by Chainlink, 30% company/team reserve held by Chainlink), but none of these allocations were governed by a published, date-stamped vesting contract — Chainlink Labs controls the company and node-operator wallets and releases tokens at its own discretion. Because there is no programmatic unlock schedule, no specific future unlock dates can be cited without fabrication.
caveats: 1) tokenunlocks.app does not list LINK as having a tracked vesting schedule on its free public pages (as of mid-2025) because there are no on-chain vesting contracts to index. 2) Chainlink's 2017 whitepaper and ICO announcement (https://link.smartcontract.com/whitepaper) describe the three-bucket allocation but state no lock-up dates. 3) Messari's free LINK profile (https://messari.io/asset/chainlink) notes the allocation split but marks vesting details as 'NOT_AVAILABLE_FREE_TIER' behind their Pro paywall. 4) CryptoRank.io free tab (https://cryptorank.io/price/chainlink/vesting) shows no scheduled future unlock events for LINK. 5) Chainlink Labs has made large discretionary transfers from its reserve wallet (e.g., the 'Build' program grants, staking pool seeding) which are visible on Etherscan (main reserve wallet: 0x98c63b7b319dfbdf3d811530f2ab9dfe4983af9d) but carry no pre-announced dates, making forward-looking unlock event rows impossible to populate honestly. Any specific unlock dates found on third-party sites are estimates or historical observations, not contractual commitments.
